description_long: A collection of variously sized cardboard parcels is stacked together, forming a compact cluster. The boxes are mostly rectangular and square, with some larger and some smaller, and they are arranged in a somewhat irregular pile. Most of the parcels are a light brown color typical of corrugated cardboard, while a couple of boxes are a pale gray, suggesting either a different type of cardboard or a label. The surfaces of the boxes show subtle creases, folds, and tape lines, indicating they have been handled or shipped. The overall structure is blocky, with flat tops and sides, and the boxes are tightly packed, leaving little space between them. This arrangement is commonly seen in urban environments such as loading docks, sidewalks, or near storefronts awaiting delivery or pickup.
description: A tightly packed group of cardboard parcels in various sizes and colors, mostly brown with a few gray boxes, stacked together in a blocky arrangement. The boxes show creases, tape, and subtle wear, typical of shipped packages.
description_view_0: Front view shows the largest boxes at the base, with several smaller parcels stacked on top and to the sides.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the stack and highlights the vertical arrangement of the parcels, with more visible layering.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the gray box more prominently and shows the irregular stacking of the parcels from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the width of the largest boxes and the staggered placement of the smaller parcels.
